descriptions: Hells Angels and Desperados were both ambushed together, suggesting cooperation.

Sadistic Souls Motorcycle Club.description: White supremacist outlaw motorcycle club founded in 2010 by ex-KKK member Dennis McGiffen. Acts as the militant wing of Aryan Nations, with chapters across the US and several other countries. Listed as an active neo-Nazi group by the Southern Poverty Law Center since 2014.

descriptions: SSMC claims to be the militant arm of Aryan Nations.
edges.evidence_quote: According to their official website, the club claims to be the "militant arm" of Aryan Nations.

descriptions: SSMC formally merged with Aryan Nations in July 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2012, SSMC formally merged with Aryan Nations.

descriptions: SSMC is part of a coalition with The Creativity Movement.
edges.evidence_quote: Their club's motto, "The Black & Silver Solution"', is the phrase given to represent a coalition of several racist organizations with the United Klans of America and The Creativity Movement.

descriptions: SSMC is part of a coalition with United Klans of America.
edges.evidence_quote: Their club's motto, "The Black & Silver Solution"', is the phrase given to represent a coalition of several racist organizations with the United Klans of America and The Creativity Movement.

descriptions: Nig Rosen, head of the 69th Street Gang, became partners with the Lucchese crime family through a business venture.
edges.evidence_quote: Rosen became partners with Gaetano "Tommy" Lucchese after buying the Sweet Valley Improvement Company which was used by the Lucchese crime family to ship clothing out of New York's garment district.

descriptions: Dogs of War is a Hells Angels puppet club.
edges.evidence_quote: a number of puppet clubs were formed such as the Dogs of War club in Niagara Falls

descriptions: The Red Knights were formed in opposition to the Klan and engaged in violent responses.
edges.evidence_quote: The Red Knights, a militant group formed in opposition to the Klan, responded violently to Klan provocations on several occasions.

descriptions: Jack Spot's organisation succeeded Sabinis after the White family.
edges.evidence_quote: his empire was taken over by the White family led by Alf White and subsequently by the organisations of Jack Spot and Billy Hill

Nuestra Familia.description: Nuestra Familia is a Mexican American prison gang founded in 1965 at the Correctional Training Facility in Soledad, California. It operates primarily in Northern California and within prison systems, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, and robbery. The gang is considered the leadership structure for the Norteños street gang and has a long-standing rivalry with the Mexican Mafia. It remains active with an estimated 250 members.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood engaged in a race war with La Nuestra Familia in 1975.
edges.evidence_quote: The Aryan Brotherhood grew quickly in the California prison system and eventually engaged in a race war in 1975 with the other prison gangs such as La Nuestra Familia, and Black Guerilla Family.

descriptions: Nuestra Familia is a rival gang of Aryan Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: Other rival gangs include the Black P. Stones, Bloods, Crips, D.C. Blacks, and Nuestra Familia.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood was at war with Nuestra Familia in 1975.
edges.evidence_quote: eventualmente comenzó una guerra racial en 1975 con las otras pandillas de la prisión como ... Nuestra Familia

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood considers Nuestra Familia a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Otras bandas rivales incluyen ... Nuestra Familia.

descriptions: Infobox lists Nuestra Familia as an ally of Bahala Na Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Nuestra Familia , True Brown Style

descriptions: Nuestra Familia is listed as a current ally of BGF in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Current: , Black Disciples , Bloods , Crips , Dead Man Incorporated , El Rukn , KUMI 415 , Norteños , Nuestra Familia

descriptions: Nuestra Familia is listed as an ally of CJNG in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Milenio Cartel , Juárez Cartel , La Línea (gang) , Caborca Cartel , Gulf Cartel , Primeiro Comando da Capital , MS-13 , Clan del Golfo , Popular Liberation Army , FARC-EP , 'Ndrangheta , Guerreros Unidos , Camorra , Nuestra Familia

descriptions: Fresno Bulldogs are rivals with Nuestra Familia.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sureños , Mexican Mafia , Norteños , Nuestra Familia

descriptions: Mexican Mafia and Nuestra Familia are primary rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: The primary rivals of the Mexican Mafia are Nuestra Familia.

descriptions: Nuestra Familia formed to protect against the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Northerners then formed the Nuestra Familia (NF) prison gang for protection from the Mexican Mafia, the Southern gang.

descriptions: Nuestra Familia was founded as a rival to Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: La búsqueda de 'La eMe' por el control total animó a muchos otros presos chicanos... a fundar secretamente una banda nueva en la prisión llamada La Nuestra Familia (NF).

descriptions: Mexican Mafia and Nuestra Familia had a prison dispute that led to the formation of Sureños and Norteños.
edges.evidence_quote: Las pretendidas raíces de estas pandillas provienen de una disputa carcelaria entre miembros de la Mafia Mexicana (La eMe) y Nuestra Familia (NF).

descriptions: Mexikanemi has a rivalry with Nuestra Familia.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Nuestra Familia , Texas Syndicate

descriptions: Mongols are allied with Nuestra Familia.
edges.evidence_quote: The club is allied with the Mexican Mafia – Sureño coalition, the Nuestra Familia – Norteño alliance, the Nazi Lowriders, and the Tijuana Cartel.

descriptions: Nazi Lowriders consider Nuestra Familia as main rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Sus principales rivales son los ... Nuestra Familia

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Latin Kings

descriptions: Nuestra Familia was founded as a result of conflict with the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The spark that led to the ongoing war between Norteños and members of the Mexican Mafia involved a situation in which a La eMe member allegedly stole a pair of shoes from a Northerner. This event put into motion the longest-running gang war in the state of California and the founding of Nuestra Familia.

descriptions: Nuestra Familia and Mexican Mafia are prison enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Nuestra Familia are prison enemies of the Southern Chicanos who consisted of La eMe, better known as the Mexican Mafia.

descriptions: Nuestra Familia and Mexican Mafia are prison enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Nuestra Familia son enemigos de prisión de los chicanos del sur, que consistían en 'La eMe', (más conocida como la Mafia Mexicana).

Yamaken-gumi.description: The Yamaken-gumi is a yakuza gang based in Kobe, Japan, founded in 1961 by Kenichi Yamamoto. It was the largest affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015, when it withdrew to form the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi. After a period of independence, it rejoined the Yamaguchi-gumi in 2021. The group has an estimated membership of around 800.

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi was a constituent group within Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi yakuza organization included Kobe -based Yamaken-gumi

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi is a major internal rival of Kodo-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Yamaken-gumi had been a major internal rival of the Kodo-kai

descriptions: Kyushu Seido-kai's president maintained a close relationship with the president of Yamaken-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: The second president Namikawa has allegedly maintained a close relationship with Kunio Inoue. Inoue is the president of the fourth-generation Yamaken-gumi

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi members attacked Oda-gumi by crashing a car into its offices.
edges.evidence_quote: The recent split between Yamaguchi-gumi and Watanabe's former Yamaken-gumi faction has again brought the Tsukasa aligned Oda-gumi into the spotlight as members of the Yamaken faction, Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, deliberately crashed a car into Oda-gumi's offices in March 2016.

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi is an internal rival of Kodo-kai within Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Yamaken-gumi had been a major internal rival of the Kodo-kai especially since Takayama and Tsukasa joined the headquarters of the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: powerful factions, including the Kobe-based Yamaken-gumi, the Osaka-based Takumi-gumi, and the Kyoyu-kai, broke away from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed a new group called the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: 4th Yama-kengumi is a subgroup within Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Kunio Inoue (assistant to the sixth-generation Yamaguchi-gumi Wakatou and fourth-generation Yama-kengumi)

descriptions: Fifth kumicho Koji Nakata was kaicho of Fifth Kenryu-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: He was kaicho of the Fifth Kenryu-kai .

descriptions: Fourth kumicho Kunio Inoue was kaicho of Fourth Kenryu-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: He was kaicho of the Fourth Kenryu-kai .

descriptions: Second kumicho Yoshinori Watanabe was 1st kaicho of Kenryu-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: He was 1st kaicho ( 会長 ) of the Kenryu-kai ( 健竜会 )

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi was a founding member and later under the umbrella of Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi from 2015 to 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Yamaken-gumi, along with several other organisations, withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi . From 2015 to 2020, it was under the umbrella of the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Third kumicho Kaneyoshi Kuwata was kaicho of Second Kenryu-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: He was kaicho of the Second Kenryu-kai .

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi (through its founder) attacked the rival Tanizaki-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: he was involved in an attack on the rival Tanizaki-gumi in September 1954

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi withdrew from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015, indicating a split.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Yamaken-gumi, along with several other organisations, withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi was the largest affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: It was the largest affiliate, followed by the Nagoya -based Kodo-kai , of the largest known yakuza syndicate in Japan, the Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015.

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi reunited with Yamaguchi-gumi in 2021, becoming its umbrella organization.
edges.evidence_quote: On 16 September 2021, the Yamaguchi-gumi announced that it will be welcoming back Yamaken-gumi members who had disunited from them in 2015. As a result, the Yamaken-gumi once again became the umbrella organisation of the Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi is a subsidiary or sub-group of Yamaguchi-gumi, as Kuwata held leadership roles in both.
edges.evidence_quote: Kaneyoshi Kuwata ... was a yakuza gang member, the fifth generation wakagashira of the Yamaguchi-gumi , was the third kumicho of the Yamaken-gumi yakuza gang

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi is an affiliate (subsidiary) of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: the largest and most powerful affiliate gang of the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi rejoined The Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi after being part of Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Yamaken-gumi, the core organisation of Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, rejoined Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi is an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Inoue is the president of the fourth-generation Yamaken-gumi , an affiliate of the largest-known Yamaguchi-gumi syndicate.

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi is a subgroup of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Yoshinori Watanabe joined the Yamaken Gumi, a subgroup of the Yamaguchi Gumi Yakuza

descriptions: Founder Kenichi Yamamoto was a former member of Yasuhara-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: He was a former member of Yasuhara-kai ( 安原会 ) .

descriptions: Jihad Council is the paramilitary wing of Hezbollah.
edges.evidence_quote: Hezbollah's paramilitary wing is the Jihad Council

Rock Machine Motorcycle Club.description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Montreal, Canada in 1986 by Salvatore Cazzetta and his brother Giovanni. It competed with the Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War (1994-2002), resulting in over 160 deaths. The original club was patched over into the Bandidos in 2001, but a second version was revived in 2008. As of 2022, it has over 120 chapters worldwide.

descriptions: Bandidos and Rock Machine are in conflict over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos have been involved in a conflict with the Rock Machine in Ulm and Neu-Ulm which started after Suat Erköse, a former Bandidos member, formed a Rock Machine chapter in the area in early 2011.

descriptions: Feud between Bandidos and Rock Machine after members defected.
edges.evidence_quote: A feud emerged between the Rock Machine and the Bandidos after several Bandidos members reportedly patched over to the Rock Machine.

descriptions: Bandidos are allied with Rock Machine in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Australian Bandidos are allied with the Diablos, Mobshitters, and Rock Machine

descriptions: The text states the Australian Bandidos are allied with Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: The Australian Bandidos are allied with the Diablos , Mobshitters and Rock Machine

descriptions: Bandidos are allied with Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: The Australian Bandidos are allied with the Diablos , Mobshitters and Rock Machine

descriptions: Bandidos formed a chapter by patching over a Rock Machine branch.
edges.evidence_quote: A second chapter was established after the club patched over a branch of the Rock Machine in Christchurch in November 2013.

descriptions: Bandidos patched over a Rock Machine chapter, absorbing it.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos established their first branch in Western Australia by patching-over a Rock Machine chapter in Perth on 20 October 2013.

descriptions: Bandidos merged with Rock Machine in 2000 to expand into Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos embarked on a failed endeavour to establish themselves in Canada after merging with the Montreal-based Rock Machine Motorcycle Club in 2000.

descriptions: Black Jackets and Rock Machine are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rock Machine , Veles gang , Black Dogs , Bandogs , 210 International , Dogpack 416 , Black Army , Holmblaaa , Kommando 210

descriptions: Rock Machine patched over Comanchero members, causing tension and conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of Rock Machine chapters in New South Wales had reported links to a Serbian organized crime group and used that influence to patch over members of the Comanchero Motorcycle Club in mid 2012, causing tension with existing Sydney Comancheros.

descriptions: The Rock Machine MC is listed as a rival of the Cotroni family.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Blass gang, Dubois gang, Rizzuto crime family, Rock Machine MC

descriptions: Dark Circle, founded by ex-Devil's Disciples, was Rock Machine's death squad.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dark Circle has often been described as Rock Machine's death squad

descriptions: The Perth Finks were patched over by Rock Machine in October 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2013 the Perth Finks were patched over by the Mongols MC and Rock Machine.

descriptions: The Rock Machine and Hells Angels were engaged in escalating violence in eastern Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: primarily because of public outcry due to escalating violence in eastern Canada between the Rock Machine and the Hells Angels' Quebec chapters

descriptions: Rock Machine led the 'Alliance to fight the Angels' against Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War.
edges.evidence_quote: The war began when the Hells Angels in Quebec began to try to establish a monopoly on street-level drug sales in Quebec. Many drug dealers and crime families resisted and established groups such as the 'Alliance to fight the Angels', led by the Rock Machine.

descriptions: Hells Angels and Rock Machine fought a violent biker war in Quebec from 1994 to 2002.
edges.evidence_quote: The Quebec Biker war between the Hells Angels and the Rock Machine began in 1994 and continued until late 2002 and claimed more than 162 lives, including innocent bystanders.

descriptions: Rock Machine is a rival gang; a former Zig Zag Crew member allied with Rock Machine insulted Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: A former member of the Zig Zag Crew, Fat Corey, who had allied himself with the Rock Machine was heard to make insulting remarks about the Hells Angels.

descriptions: The two groups were engaged in a violent gang war from 1994 to 2002.
edges.evidence_quote: The Quebec Biker War ... was a gang war in Montreal ... between the Quebec branch of the Hells Angels and the Rock Machine.

descriptions: The two groups were engaged in a gang war from 1994 to 2002.
edges.evidence_quote: The Quebec Biker War ... was a gang war in Montreal ... between the Quebec branch of the Hells Angels and the Rock Machine.

descriptions: The Hells Angels and Rock Machine were engaged in a violent gang war from 1994 to 2002.
edges.evidence_quote: The Quebec Biker War ... was a gang war in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, lasting from 1994 to 2002, between the Quebec branch of the Hells Angels and the Rock Machine.

descriptions: Hells Angels and Rock Machine were rivals in a gang war over drug turf.
edges.evidence_quote: Territory of the Hells Angels and Rock Machine began to overlap with one another which incited much conflict between the two before a large-scale gang war broke in 1994 .

descriptions: Some Rock Machine members joined Hells Angels in a mass patch-over.
edges.evidence_quote: On 29 December 2000, most of the Ontario biker gangs in a mass 'patch over' ... joined the Hells Angels. ... 179 bikers from the Satan's Choice, Para-Dice Riders, the Last Chance, the Lobos, the Outlaws and the Rock Machine joined the Hells Angels

descriptions: The Loners allied with the Rock Machine against the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: the Loners Motorcycle Club aligned themselves with fellow Canadian motorcycle club.

descriptions: Infobox lists Rock Machine MC as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Nations, Bandidos MC, Black Pistons MC, Breed MC, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Detroit Partnership, Grim Reapers MC, Gypsy Joker MC, Hammerskins, New Orleans crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Rock Machine MC

descriptions: The Outlaws maintain an alliance with the Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: They also maintain an alliance with fellow Hells Angels rivals, the Rock Machine

descriptions: Pagans supported Rock Machine as a rival to Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: It also found the idea of supporting Hells Angels rivals beneficial, such as the Rock Machine Motorcycle Club ; a Canadian-based international motorcycle club, it has fought several conflicts with the Hells Angels including the Quebec Biker War

descriptions: Rebels and Rock Machine engaged in a violent feud with firebombings, assaults, and assassination attempts.
edges.evidence_quote: The defection of Rebels MC members to the Rock Machine MC sparked an ongoing violent feud between the groups, when the Rock Machine settled in Perth in 2009 there were allegations by media that a turf war broke out between the two rival motorcycle clubs, with exchanges between the two groups including firebombings, assaults and the assassination attempt in 2011 of Rebels WA president Nick Martin, who survived being shot, tensions remain ongoing.

descriptions: Rizzuto crime family mediated a truce between Hells Angels and Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: the Rizzuto crime family ... seek a truce between the warring biker gangs

descriptions: Rock Machine and Bandidos became rivals after the Shedden Massacre and clashed in Germany in 2011.
edges.evidence_quote: Tensions between the Bandidos and Rock Machine started in the mid-2000s, with the Shedden Massacre and the dissolution of the Bandidos Canada driving a wedge between the reemerging Rock Machine and their former ally. The rivalry escalated into conflict in 2011, when the two clubs clashed for control of the German city of Ulm.

descriptions: Rock Machine sought help from Bandidos during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: Rock Machine sought international help from The Bandidos

descriptions: Rock Machine sought help from The Bandidos during the conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Rock Machine sought international help from The Bandidos and local drug dealers.

descriptions: Rock Machine sought help from The Bandidos during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: Rock Machine sought international help from The Bandidos and local drug dealers.

descriptions: Rock Machine sought to join the Bandidos to gain support in the war against Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Rock Machine seeks help from the Bandidos ... Faucher decided the best hope for the Rock Machine was to have the club absorbed into the Bandidos

descriptions: The Rock Machine aligned with and was eventually absorbed into the Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 1999 the Rock Machine Motorcycle Club became a "hang-around" club for the Bandidos.

descriptions: The Rock Machine became a probationary club of the Bandidos in 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: On December 1, 2000, the Rock Machine Motorcycle Club became an official probationary club of the Bandidos.

descriptions: Rock Machine merged with Bandidos in 2001.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rock Machine merges with the Bandidos in 2001

descriptions: Rock Machine became a probationary chapter of the Bandidos, eventually patching over.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rock Machine motorcycle club gained the status of a 'hang-around' club in May 1999 and after eighteen months, became a probationary chapter of the Bandidos Motorcycle Club on 1 December 2000.

descriptions: Rock Machine patched over to become a chapter of the Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: the Rock Machine agreed to join the Bandidos Motorcycle Club based in Texas

descriptions: Rock Machine became a probationary chapter of Bandidos in 2000 and full-patch in 2001.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rock Machine Motorcycle Club gained the status of a "hang-around" club in May 1999 and after eighteen months, became a probationary chapter of the Bandidos Motorcycle Club on 1 December 2000.

descriptions: Rock Machine patched over to become members of Bandidos Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: Regardless of the prior issues, on 1 December 2001, the Rock Machine Motorcycle Club became official members of the Bandidos Canada in a "patch-over" ceremony at the Rock Machine's Kingston chapter club house.

descriptions: The Rock Machine merged with the Bandidos, ceasing to exist as a separate entity.
edges.evidence_quote: the Rock Machine had merged with the Bandidos and was no longer active at the time.

descriptions: Rock Machine killed a member of the Cotroni crime family to gain favor with the Rizzutos.
edges.evidence_quote: a team of Rock Machine killers ... gunned down Paolo Cotroni ... partly to gain the favor of the Rizzutos

descriptions: The Rock Machine killed a Cotroni family member to gain favor with the Rizzutos.
edges.evidence_quote: Cotroni was a member of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Cotroni crime family who were the rivals of the Sicilian Mafia Rizzuto crime family. Cotroni was killed partly to gain the favor of the Rizzutos and partly because he was a friend of Boucher.

descriptions: Dark Circle member employed a hitman who also worked for Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: Gallant was employed by a member of the Dark Circle and also frequently carried out contracts for the Rock Machine

descriptions: Rock Machine formed the Alliance with Dark Circle to fight Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The local Rock Machine Motorcycle Club formed an affiliation, the Alliance, with Montreal crime families such as the Pelletier Clan, Dark Circle and other independent dealers who wished to resist the Hells Angels' attempts to establish a monopoly on street-level drug trade in the city.

descriptions: The Dark Circle was allied with the Rock Machine against the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The resulting investigation first alerted the public to the existence of the Dark Circle, and it was reported that the Hells Angels would pay well for information identifying the members of the Dark Circle.

descriptions: Rock Machine killed a member of Death Riders MC, initiating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: three members of the Rock Machine entered a business in downtown Montreal. They assassinated Pierre Daoust, a member of a Hells Angel support club, the Death Riders Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: Devils Disciples former members joined the Alliance alongside Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: former members of the Devils Disciples , resisted the Hells Angels' attempts at monopolisation and established a coalition known as the 'Alliance to fight the Angels'.

descriptions: Rock Machine founder Salvatore Cazzetta had contacts with the Dubois Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Cazzetta spoke fluent French, English and Italian and had contacts with the Rizzuto crime family, the West End Gang, and the Dubois Gang.

descriptions: Many Rock Machine members were formerly in the Executeurs.
edges.evidence_quote: The majority of the Rock Machine were former members of the Merciless Riders, the Executeurs and the SS.

descriptions: Rock Machine allied with Gremium MC during European expansion.
edges.evidence_quote: During the club's expansion into Europe, it made an alliance with Gremium MC, one of the largest motorcycle clubs in Germany.

descriptions: Rock Machine and Hells Angels were rivals in the Quebec Biker War, competing for drug trade control.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rock Machine competed with the Hells Angels for control of the street-level narcotics trade in Quebec.

descriptions: The two clubs were rivals in the Quebec Biker War over drug trade control.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rock Machine competed with the Hells Angels for control of the street-level narcotics trade in Quebec.

descriptions: Rock Machine and Hells Angels were rivals in the Quebec Biker War.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rock Machine competed with the Hells Angels for control of the street-level narcotics trade in Quebec.

descriptions: The two clubs were rivals in the Quebec Biker War, fighting for drug trade control.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rock Machine competed with the Hells Angels for control of the street-level narcotics trade in Quebec.

descriptions: A ceasefire was brokered by the Rizzuto family between the Rock Machine and Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The truce had an ulterior motive for the Hells Angels, as they hoped to halt the expansion of the Bandidos in Canada, especially Ontario.

descriptions: Rock Machine allied with Loners Motorcycle Club in the 2000s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 2000s, the Rock Machine allied themselves with fellow international Canadian motorcycle club the Loners Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: The Loners aligned with the Rock Machine in Ontario.
edges.evidence_quote: The Loners had aligned themselves with the Rock Machine, holding a party in Toronto in June 2000 that was attended by dozens of "machinists".

descriptions: Rock Machine formed an alliance with Loners MC in the 2010s.
edges.evidence_quote: A fellow Canadian-based international motorcycle club, an alliance between the two was established in the 2010s.

descriptions: Rock Machine formed an alliance with Manitoba Warriors in 2008.
edges.evidence_quote: A Native-Canadian gang based in the Canada's prairies, an alliance was formed between the two groups during Rock Machine's expansion into Western Canada in 2008.

descriptions: Many Rock Machine members were formerly in the Merciless Riders.
edges.evidence_quote: The majority of the Rock Machine were former members of the Merciless Riders, the Executeurs and the SS.

descriptions: Disgruntled Mongols members joined the reestablished Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rock Machine was reestablished by former Bandidos, and disgruntled members of the Mongols Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: The Rock Machine recruited former Outlaws members in Ontario.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 2000, the Rock Machine set up in Ontario began recruiting former members of the Outlaws Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: Outlaws supported Rock Machine during Quebec Biker War and later formed official alliance in 2011.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws motorcycle club had initially given minor support to the Rock Machine during the Quebec Biker War, The Outlaws were also fighting a conflict with the Hell's Angels during this period. An official alliance would be formed between the resurrected Rock Machine and the Outlaws in Manitoba in 2011.

descriptions: Pagans met with Rock Machine in 2011 to establish a friendship.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2011, dozens of the Pagans traveled to the Canadian province of New Brunswick to meet with the several chapters of the Rock Machine and other locals.

descriptions: The Rock Machine conspired with Palmers MC to murder a Hells Angels leader.
edges.evidence_quote: Prominent Rock Machine member Serge Cyr was placed under arrest and charged with conspiracy along with 13 others including members of the Rock Machine, Pelletier Clan and Palmers MC for planning the murder of Hells Angels president Maurice "Mom" Boucher.

descriptions: Palmers MC is a puppet club of the Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: the Rock Machine created the Palmers MC, a Rock Machine puppet club

descriptions: Palmers MC was created as a support club for Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: This led to the creation of the Palmers MC, a Rock Machine MC support club with chapters in Montreal and Quebec City.

descriptions: Rock Machine formed the Alliance with Pelletier Clan to fight Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The local Rock Machine Motorcycle Club formed an affiliation, the Alliance, with Montreal crime families such as the Pelletier Clan, Dark Circle and other independent dealers who wished to resist the Hells Angels' attempts to establish a monopoly on street-level drug trade in the city.

descriptions: The Rock Machine conspired with the Pelletier Clan to murder a Hells Angels leader.
edges.evidence_quote: Prominent Rock Machine member Serge Cyr was placed under arrest and charged with conspiracy along with 13 others including members of the Rock Machine, Pelletier Clan and Palmers MC for planning the murder of Hells Angels president Maurice "Mom" Boucher.

descriptions: Rock Machine and Rebels MC became rivals after Rock Machine expanded into Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivalry between the Rock Machine and the Rebels Motorcycle Club started when the Canadian club expanded into Australia.

descriptions: Red Devils, as Hells Angels support club, have had incidents with Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: the Red Devils are the official support club of the Hells Angels, they have had several incidents with the Rock Machine.

descriptions: A Rizzuto capo supported the Rock Machine by selling them drugs.
edges.evidence_quote: one of the Rizzuto capos, Paolo Gervasi was a supporter of the Rock Machine, selling them drugs

descriptions: Rock Machine founder Salvatore Cazzetta had contacts with the Rizzuto crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Cazzetta spoke fluent French, English and Italian and had contacts with the Rizzuto crime family, the West End Gang, and the Dubois Gang.

descriptions: The Rock Machine killed a Cotroni to gain favor with the Rizzuto crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Cotroni was killed partly to gain the favor of the Rizzutos and partly because he was a friend of Boucher.

descriptions: Vendettas MC is a support club created by Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: A RMMC created support club that was founded in Winnipeg, Manitoba. Serves as an international support club for the Rock Machine with chapters in Canada, Australia and Russia.

descriptions: Rock Machine member Cazzetta arranged a drug deal with West End Gang member Richard Matticks.
edges.evidence_quote: Cazzetta introduced the informer to Richard Matticks, the brother of Gerald Matticks of the West End Gang.

descriptions: Rock Machine founder Salvatore Cazzetta had contacts with the West End Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Cazzetta spoke fluent French, English and Italian and had contacts with the Rizzuto crime family, the West End Gang, and the Dubois Gang.

descriptions: WolfSSChanze is a splinter faction of Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: WolfSSChanze Faction - a splinter faction of the Rock Machine led by Suat Erköse.

descriptions: Rockers MC was involved in the war against Rock Machine, carrying out attacks.
edges.evidence_quote: In amidst of the war with Rock Machine, Rockers MC biker Paul "Fon Fon" Fontaine, along with an accomplice, murdered provincial prison guard Pierre Rondeau ...

descriptions: West End Gang is allied with Rock Machine MC per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Rock Machine MC

descriptions: Clan Serra was a satellite group allied with Clan Alfieri.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvatore Serra , da Pagani ... figlioccio di Carmine Alfieri , capo di un gruppo satellite

descriptions: Clan Serra was in conflict with the Di Maio clan.
edges.evidence_quote: in lotta col clan del cutoliano Salvatore Di Maio

descriptions: Serra clan was part of the anti-Cutolo alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Altre famiglie coinvolte nell'ampia alleanza anticutoliana furono ... i Serra e Olivieri di Pagani

descriptions: The Parisi clan and Palermiti clan merged in 2017 to form a joint criminal society to fight against a splinter group.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2017 al clan Parisi si è unito il clan Palermiti, dando vita a una 'società della guerra', nata per sostenere il conflitto scoppiato nel quartiere contro la compagine 'scissionista' capeggiata da Antonio Busco

descriptions: The Palermiti clan merged with the Parisi clan to form a joint criminal society.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 2017, the Palermiti clan has joined the Parisi clan, giving life to a 'war society'

descriptions: Locale di Africo is a 'Ndrangheta local unit involved in cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono stati sequestrati nel 2007 250 chili di cocaina provenienti dal Sud America e in transito a Dakar in Senegal che sarebbero dovuti arrivare in Italia per conto dei Morabito e della Locale di Africo

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to these successor gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9" All of these crews, excepting the "Be Like Boys" gang, are named after Detroit city streets or housing projects and some of these gangs still exist under new leadership.

descriptions: Infobox lists Springfield crime family as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family , Chicago Outfit , Milwaukee crime family , Springfield crime family

descriptions: Rockford consigliere Joseph Zito was brother to Springfield boss Frank Zito.
edges.evidence_quote: Zammuto's consigliere Joseph Zito was a brother to the Springfield Mafia boss Frank Zito.

descriptions: Dooney Boys is listed as a rival of 39ers gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Dooney Boys

descriptions: The Ramashish Koeri gang cooperated with these four gangs in committing murders.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang, along with four other caste-based gangs led by Ghamari Karwar, Rambachan Yadav, Triveni Kahar, and Gaurik was responsible for over hundred murders in the Kaimur region.

descriptions: PKK came to agreements with PPSF to facilitate activities.
edges.evidence_quote: The PKK also managed to come to agreements with the Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine (DFLP), the Fatah of Yassir Arafat or the Palestinian Popular Struggle Front (PPSF) of Samir Ghawshah and also with Masoud Barzani's Kurdistan Democratic Party (KDP) in Iraqi Kurdistan which would facilitate their activities.

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: PPSF split from PFLP.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1967, Palestinian Popular Struggle Front (PPSF) broke away from the PFLP.

edges.source: Communist Party of Peru – Red Mantaro Base Committee
edges.target: Communist Party of Ecuador – Red Sun
descriptions: PCP-CBMR has signed documents with Communist Party of Ecuador – Red Sun, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The group has in the past signed documents with the Communist Party of Ecuador – Red Sun.

Shining Path.description: Far-left political party and guerrilla group in Peru, founded in 1969 by Abimael Guzmán. It launched a 'people's war' in 1980 aiming to overthrow the government and establish a New Democracy through guerrilla warfare. The group is designated as a terrorist organization by Peru, the US, EU, Japan, and Canada. Since the capture of its leaders in the 1990s and 2012, its activity has declined, with remaining factions operating in the VRAEM region.

edges.target: Communist Party of Peru – Huallaga Regional Committee
descriptions: PCP-CRH was a faction of Shining Path active in the Huallaga region.
edges.evidence_quote: the Communist Party of Peru – Huallaga Regional Committee ( PCP-CRH ) was active at the Huallaga region from 2004 until Comrade Artemio's capture in 2012.

edges.target: Communist Party of Peru – Huallaga Regional Committee
descriptions: PCP-CRH is a splinter group of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: The three remaining splinter groups based themselves in the VRAEM area: ... - The "Communist Party of Peru – Huallaga Regional Committee" (PCP-CRH), a collective established in 2004 in the Huallaga Valley led by Comrade Artemio.

edges.target: Communist Party of Peru – Red Mantaro Base Committee
descriptions: PCP-CBMR is a faction of Shining Path operating in the Mantaro Valley.
edges.evidence_quote: In addition to the MPCP, the Communist Party of Peru – Red Mantaro Base Committee ( PCP-CBMR ) has been operating in the Mantaro Valley since 2001

edges.target: Communist Party of Peru – Red Mantaro Base Committee
descriptions: PCP-CBMR is a splinter group of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: The three remaining splinter groups based themselves in the VRAEM area: ... - The "Communist Party of Peru – Red Mantaro Base Committee" (PCP-CBMR), established in 2001 as a base committee in the Mantaro Valley led by a "Comrade Netzel López Lozano".

descriptions: FUDEPP is a political wing branch of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: It has two main branches, MOVADEF (2009–2024) and FUDEPP

descriptions: MPCP is the main remaining faction of Shining Path, but distanced itself from its legacy.
edges.evidence_quote: The main remaining faction of the Shining Path, the Militarized Communist Party of Peru ( MPCP ), is active in the VRAEM region of Peru, and it has since distanced itself from the Shining Path's legacy in 2018

descriptions: MPCP is a splinter group of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: The main remaining group, called the Militarized Communist Party of Peru (MPCP) of about 300 individuals remained in the Valle de los Ríos Apurímac, Ene y Mantaro (VRAEM) region, reportedly making revenue by escorting cocaine traffickers and are reportedly led by two brothers; Víctor and Jorge Quispe Palomino.

descriptions: MOVADEF is a political wing branch of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: It has two main branches, MOVADEF (2009–2024) and FUDEPP

descriptions: EGP is the paramilitary wing of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: The People's Guerrilla Army ( Ejército Guerrillero Popular , EGP ) was officially created on 3 December 1982 for the purposes of combat, mobilisation and producing an income for the group.

descriptions: People's Guerrilla Army is a splinter group of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: The three remaining splinter groups based themselves in the VRAEM area: - The "People's Guerrilla Army" (ERP) led by the Quispe Palomino brothers.

descriptions: Shining Path split from Peruvian Communist Party – Red Flag.
edges.evidence_quote: Split from: Peruvian Communist Party – Red Flag

descriptions: Shining Path split from the Peruvian Communist Party – Red Flag.
edges.evidence_quote: It was an offshoot of the Peruvian Communist Party – Red Flag

descriptions: EPL is the name of the paramilitary wing under the Mantaro faction since 2001.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 2001, it has been operated by the Mantaro faction under the name of People's Liberation Army ( Ejército Popular de Liberación , EPL ).

descriptions: A Shining Path faction allegedly allied with FARC in the early 2000s.
edges.evidence_quote: The group had allegedly made an alliance with the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) in the early 2000s, learning how to use rockets against aircraft.

descriptions: Sendero Rojo was a faction that rejected Guzmán's peace treaty and collapsed, leading to MPCP's formation.
edges.evidence_quote: the Militarized Communist Party of Peru (MPCP) is considered the group's main successor, founded in 1999 by brothers Víctor and Jorge Quispe Palomino after the collapse of Sendero Rojo , the faction that had rejected Guzmán's peace treaty.

descriptions: Sendero Rojo is a splinter faction that broke away from Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: Guzmán's role as the leader of the Shining Path was taken over by Óscar Ramírez ("Comrade Feliciano"), who established Sendero Rojo (or PCP Pro-Seguir), aiming to reorganise the party and to continue the armed struggle while breaking with Guzmán, but not with his ideology.

descriptions: Shining Path fought against MRTA.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shining Path began to fight against Peru's other major guerrilla group, the Túpac Amaru Revolutionary Movement (MRTA)

descriptions: Taliban forms alliances with People's Liberation Army.
edges.evidence_quote: The Taliban form alliances with gangs ( People's Liberation Army and the Group of 41 ) of the Kalenjin tribe

descriptions: Gioffrè killed Diego Spinella, a member of the Spinella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1993 viene ucciso dai Gioffrè Diego Spinella, esponente dell'omonima cosca.

descriptions: Keane-Collopy gang is in conflict with the Ryan Gang as part of the Limerick feud.
edges.evidence_quote: The feud, which mainly involves the Keane-Collopys, the Ryans and the McCarthy-Dundon gang

descriptions: The McCarthy-Dundon Gang and the Ryan Gang are both participants in the Limerick feud, which is a conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The feud, which involves several criminal families, mainly the McCarthy-Dundons, the Ryans and the Keane-Collopy gang from St. Mary's Park, has been ongoing since 2000.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Ryan Gang as an ally of the McCarthy-Dundon Gang, despite the text describing them as participants in the same feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Hutch Organised Crime Gang, Ryan Gang

descriptions: Los Granados is a splinter group of Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Granados en las municipalidades de Ixtapa y Zihuatanejo en el Estado de Guerrero.

descriptions: Turkish Islamic Federation has links to the Grey Wolves.
edges.evidence_quote: Organizations such as Turkish Federation Netherlands (Turkse Federatie Nederland, TFN) and Turkish Islamic Federation (Turks Islamitische Federatie) have links to the Grey Wolves.

descriptions: The Matranga family was an early predecessor and rival to the Los Angeles crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The early years of organized crime in California were marked by the division of various Italian street gangs, such as the Black Hand organizations, in the early 20th century. The most prominent of these was the Matranga family.
